topic: post-harvest grape cooling and volatile aroma protection
trigger_keywords: enfriar la uva tras la cosecha

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Warm grapes are cooled promptly after harvest and before processing.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Lower temperature slows oxidation, microbial activity, and the loss or transformation of volatile aroma compounds.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: More primary volatile aromas can be retained for the intended fermentation and wine style.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Prompt cooling after harvest protects volatile primary aromas by slowing oxidation and biological activity.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
